Lab Report — Dynamic Testing
Environment Setup

Module 35 · Prepare Lab Environment for Dynamic Testing

Student Aadith CH
Date 2026-06-04
Host aadith-LOQ-15IAX9
Emulator Genymotion v3.10.0
Status ✔ Complete
🎯 Lab Objectives
  • Task A: Configure an Android emulator (Genymotion) with ADB debugging and root access
  • Task B: Install required tooling — Frida server on device, Frida + Objection on host, Burp Suite
  • Task C: Configure network proxying so all app traffic routes through Burp Suite for inspection
  • Task D: Document environment setup steps and perform verification checks
TASK A

Android Emulator & ADB Debugging

1
Launch Genymotion Android Emulator
Opened Genymotion Desktop PRO v3.10.0 and booted the virtual Android phone (570×1230, 220dpi). The emulator uses a VirtualBox x86 backend (vbox86p), which allows root access by default.
Genymotion Phone — Home Screen
Fig 1.1 — Genymotion emulator running successfully. Home screen visible with Google search bar.
✓
Observed: Emulator booted successfully. Device is responsive, network connectivity active (Wi-Fi icon visible in status bar).
2
Verify ADB Device Connection
Ran adb devices on the host machine to confirm the emulator is recognized over TCP on port 6555.
$ adb devices List of devices attached 127.0.0.1:6555 device
Terminal — adb devices
Fig 1.2 — ADB detects the Genymotion emulator at 127.0.0.1:6555.
✓
Observed: Device listed as 127.0.0.1:6555 device, confirming ADB connection over localhost TCP.
3
Confirm Root Access via ADB Shell
Opened an ADB shell and ran whoami to verify the shell session has root privileges — a prerequisite for running Frida server and performing dynamic instrumentation.
$ adb shell vbox86p:/ # whoami root
Terminal — adb shell whoami
Fig 1.3 — whoami returns "root", confirming full root access inside the emulator shell.
✓
Observed: whoami → root. The emulator shell runs as root, required for placing and running the Frida server in /data/local/tmp/.
4
Verify Android Version and Device IP
Navigated to Settings → About Phone to record the Android version and the device IP address, which is needed for proxy configuration.
Genymotion — Settings → About Phone (Android 11)
Fig 1.4a — Android version 11, IP address 10.0.3.16 on first emulator instance.
Genymotion — Settings → About Phone (Android 12)
Fig 1.4b — Android version 12 (second instance). IP address confirmed as 10.0.3.16.
✓
Observed: Device IP is 10.0.3.16. Genymotion uses 10.0.3.2 as the host-facing gateway — this is the proxy target address for Burp Suite.
5
Confirm Superuser App Present
Checked the app drawer to confirm the Superuser application is installed — this manages root permission grants to apps on the emulator.
Genymotion — App Drawer
Fig 1.5 — App drawer showing Superuser app installed, confirming the emulator is rooted.
✓
Observed: Superuser app visible in drawer. Also noted WebView Shell app — useful for testing WebView traffic interception.
TASK B

Install Required Tooling

6
Install Frida and Objection on Host (Python venv)
Inside a Python virtual environment (venv), installed frida-tools and objection via pip. Verified both tools are functional.
(venv) $ frida --version 17.10.1 (venv) $ objection --help Runtime Mobile Exploration by: @leonjza from @sensepost
Terminal — Frida version + Objection help
Fig 2.1 — Frida v17.10.1 confirmed. Objection help output visible below.
✓
Observed: frida 17.10.1 installed. Objection loads correctly and shows all available commands including patchapk, start, run.
7
Review Objection Command Reference
Ran objection --help to review all available flags and sub-commands before beginning device-side operations.
Terminal — objection --help (full output)
Fig 2.2 — Full objection help output. Key flags: -g (gadget/app name), -N (network), -s (spawn). Commands: patchapk, start, run.
✓
Observed: Objection supports patchapk (embed frida-gadget into APK), android sslpinning disable (via start command), and network connection mode via -N flag.
8
Deploy Frida Server on Device
Downloaded the correct Frida server binary for x86 architecture (matching the Genymotion vbox86p emulator), pushed it to the device, set executable permissions, and launched it in the background using nohup. Verified it is running and reachable from the host using frida-ps -U.
$ adb push frida-server /data/local/tmp/frida-server $ adb shell chmod 755 /data/local/tmp/frida-server $ adb shell "nohup /data/local/tmp/frida-server >/dev/null 2>&1 &" [1] 3653 # Verify process is running $ adb shell ps | grep frida root 3653 1 ... frida-server # List processes via Frida from host (venv) $ frida-ps -U PID Name ---- -------- 2596 Gallery 2364 Messaging 2401 Phone 3230 Search 2746 Settings 2814 Superuser 3501 WebView Shell
Terminal — Frida server running + frida-ps -U
Fig 2.3 — frida-server confirmed running (PID 3653). frida-ps -U lists all running processes on the device, confirming host-to-device Frida communication is working.
✓
Observed: frida-server PID 3653 confirmed via ps | grep frida. frida-ps -U successfully lists device processes — Frida server is fully operational and communicating with the host.
9
Configure Burp Suite Proxy Listener
Opened Burp Suite Community Edition v2026.2.3. Navigated to Proxy → Proxy Settings and configured a proxy listener on *:8080 (all interfaces) to accept traffic from the emulator.
Burp Suite — Proxy Settings → Listeners
Fig 2.4 — Burp Suite proxy listener running on *:8080 with HTTP/2 support enabled. CA certificate export option visible for device installation.
✓
Observed: Listener running on *:8080 (all interfaces). Burp CA certificate available for export — required to intercept HTTPS traffic without certificate errors.
TASK C

Configure Network Proxying Through Burp

10
Set Proxy in Genymotion Network Settings
In Genymotion Desktop, navigated to Settings → Network and enabled HTTP proxy pointing to 10.0.3.2:8080. In Genymotion, 10.0.3.2 is the host machine's gateway address from the emulator's perspective.
Genymotion — Settings → Network → Proxy
Fig 3.1 — Genymotion proxy configured: Type HTTP, Host 10.0.3.2, Port 8080. This routes all emulator traffic to Burp on the host.
✓
Observed: Proxy set to 10.0.3.2:8080. Genymotion uses 10.0.3.2 as the host NAT gateway, so all device HTTP/S traffic will now pass through Burp.
11
Set Global HTTP Proxy via ADB
As an additional system-level configuration, set the Android global HTTP proxy using adb shell settings. This ensures all apps (not just browsers) route through Burp. Verified the setting was applied correctly.
$ adb shell settings put global http_proxy 10.0.3.2:8080 $ adb shell settings get global http_proxy 10.0.3.2:8080
Terminal — ADB Proxy Configuration
Fig 3.2 — Global HTTP proxy set and verified via ADB: 10.0.3.2:8080 confirmed active.
✓
Observed: settings get global http_proxy returns 10.0.3.2:8080 — proxy is applied system-wide at the Android settings level.
12
Verify Traffic Captured in Burp HTTP History
Opened the WebView Browser Tester on the emulator and navigated to https://www.google.com. Switched to Burp Suite's Proxy → HTTP history tab to confirm traffic is being intercepted.
Burp Suite — HTTP History showing captured traffic
Fig 3.3 — Burp HTTP history showing a GET request to http://www.google.com captured from the emulator, confirming the proxy is working.
Genymotion + Burp Suite — Side by Side View
Fig 3.4 — Genymotion browser navigating to Google (left) while Burp captures the HTTP request in real time (right). Proxy interception confirmed end-to-end.
✓
Observed: HTTP request to http://www.google.com visible in Burp history (GET /m?hl=en&source=android-launcher-widget&q=example.com). Traffic successfully routed through Burp proxy from the emulator.
TASK D

Verification Checks

13
Final Frida Process List Verification
Ran frida-ps -U one final time to confirm the complete setup: Frida server is running on the device, host tools are working, and the USB/TCP bridge is stable.
Terminal — Final frida-ps -U output
Fig 4.1 — Final frida-ps -U output listing all processes on the device. Complete toolchain verified: emulator ↔ ADB ↔ Frida server ↔ host tools all operational.
✓
Observed: All device processes listed successfully via frida-ps -U. The entire dynamic analysis toolchain is confirmed working end-to-end.
